anchor_patterns.convention: \b(the convention (here|in this repo|is)|by convention|we conventionally|the pattern here is|we always|we never)\b
anchor_patterns.moved_away: \b(we moved away from|we stopped using|we no longer|we dropped|we deprecated|we replaced .{0,40} with)\b
anchor_patterns.prior_attempt: \b(we (tried|attempted) (that|this|it)|that was tried|we already tried|has been tried before)\b
anchor_patterns.prior_decision: \b(as (we|previously) (decided|agreed)|we decided|the decision was|we settled on|we standardi[sz]ed on)\b
anchor_patterns.reverted: \b(was reverted|reverted because|had to revert|we reverted|this broke|caused an (outage|incident|regression))\b

disclosure_T7: Repository and cutoff selection is not blinded. The founder chose these repositories and this cutoff. The reasons are recorded above, including the known bias risk of each choice.

pattern_revisions.effect_on_existing_anchors: Anchors mined before this change are unaffected on disk. Re-run `mine` to apply it; several cockroachdb anchors matched only on this alternative and will drop out.
pattern_revisions.reason: The bare `we always` / `we never` alternatives matched forward-looking review requests — e.g. 'assert that we never see an event that is older than the checkpoint' — which are proposals about future code, not references to an established convention. Requiring the present-perfect ('we have never', "we've always") restricts the match to statements about existing practice, which is what the pattern was for.

provenance_note: Anchors were mined with an earlier version of ANCHOR_PATTERNS, then re-filtered in place against the corrected patterns (see pattern_revisions and refilter). Counts below are POST-refilter and are the ones consistent with the code. 13 of the 24 originally mined anchors were false positives of the loose `convention` alternative.

refilter.limitation: Matching is against the stored (truncated) text, not the original comment body. An anchor whose only match lay past MAX_ANCHOR_CHARS is dropped here though the miner would have kept it; 1 anchors are at the truncation limit and could be affected.
refilter.method: re-applied current ANCHOR_PATTERNS to stored anchor text; no new API calls

repositories.selection_reason_T7: Chosen for a maintained ADR directory (docs/architecture-decisions), created 2020-01 so >3 years of history, and >20k merged PRs with substantive review threads. Risk: it is a plugin framework, so much of its decision history is about extension points rather than runtime behaviour, which may bias tasks toward API-shape questions.

Chosen for docs/RFCS — an unusually complete written record of rejected alternatives, which is exactly the C-exp evidence class — created 2014-02, and a strong culture of documented reverts. Risk: database internals are specialised enough that a model's parametric knowledge of CockroachDB may be thin, which could flatter every memory arm equally. The A0 filter is the control for this.

Chosen as a third, smaller repository (7k stars vs 32k/34k) to test whether the effect survives outside very large projects, with docs/rfcs present and a specification-driven review culture that produces explicit 'we decided X because Y' comments. Risk: it is governed by an external specification, so some 'decisions' are inherited rather than made locally.
